mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in 60% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 250,000 signals robust economic expansion that boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of months where payroll growth exceeds the 250,000 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, producing positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio over 60-day forward windows or fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by 1% annually.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production signals immediate supply-side bottlenecks that compress short-term corporate margins and trigger negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in 70% of industrial production contraction periods or the signal loses statistical significance during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 2.5% year-over-year, inflation expectations force markets to price in tighter monetary policy, generating negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns are positive in over 65% of months where CPI inflation exceeds the 2.5% threshold, invalidating the inflation-aversion channel.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment combined with stagnant payroll growth indicates labor market deterioration that suppresses consumer spending forecasts and drives negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces positive 60-day returns in 60% of observed cases or shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate revenue projections, creating immediate positive price pressure on the S&P 500 over the following trading week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in 55% of quarters where GDP growth is positive, or the correlation coefficient drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in industrial production exceeding 0.5% during low unemployment regimes signals supply-side overheating that triggers negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in 60% of triggered instances or the signal fails to predict direction during low unemployment regimes.
